governing_requirement: Publish trusted-signed, idempotent, lossless invalidation events with exact homogeneous targets, exact reason/action mapping, effective time, rollback or supersession evidence, append-only journal position, and deterministic rejection of replay, collision, fork, deletion, reorder, stale trust, and revocation drift.
prerequisites: MF-P6-08.07 remains blocked because no current-state operational certificate invalidation decision exists.
MF-P6-10.05 remains blocked because no live adoption conformance decision exists.
verified_foundations: Strict closed invalidation schema with canonical payload hash, Ed25519 producer_journal signature, production trust-key checks, fixture/runtime evidence firewall, occurred/effective timestamps, and blocking severity.
Exactly 27 invalidation reasons equal the adoption revalidation-trigger set.
Each reason restricts target kinds and requires a closed minimum action set.
Exact target and transition IDs are unique within an event, every transition strictly lowers authority, and active certificate invalidation retains an explicit terminal status.
Required actions reference known transitions, cover every transition, carry deadlines and verification-policy hashes, and include the reason's minimum action set.
Release supersession requires an exact replacement binding and non-supersession events reject that binding.

The separate signed bridge lifecycle chain detects duplicate event IDs, noncontiguous sequence, stream/epoch drift, previous-hash/causation drift, nonmonotonic time, illegal transitions, journal replay mismatch, and invalidation-driven state changes without an exact invalidation-document binding.
Focused taxonomy, lifecycle replay, trust-role/revocation, Ruff, and schema parsing validations pass.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Use a valid trusted-signed release_revoked event with rollback_binding=null.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: Release revocation requires rollback_release as an action but does not require or resolve exact rollback bytes/evidence.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: One qa_regression event carries both artifact and certificate transitions, with all actions covering both.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: Permitted kinds are checked independently; an event is not required to contain one homogeneous target kind/scope class.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Add refresh_release to a certificate_revoked event in addition to its required actions.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: The policy is enforced as a subset, not an exact action set; an undeclared extra action can acquire signed execution authority.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Set sequence=999 and causation_id=invented-causation, then trusted re-sign.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: The standalone invalidation validator accepts declared journal position without the prior event, expected stream/head, or checkpoint.

trusted_resign_probes.mutation: Two different valid trusted-signed invalidation payloads use the same idempotency key.
trusted_resign_probes.gap: Validation is single-event only; no invalidation batch/ledger validator rejects same-key/different-body collisions or proves same-key/same-body replay safety.

confirmed_gaps: No production invalidation publisher or append-only invalidation ledger exists.
No batch/chain validator binds invalidation sequence, causation, stream, idempotency key, prior payload hash, checkpoint, or current revocation head.
Reason target policy permits heterogeneous target kinds and action policy permits extras rather than exact equality.
Rollback binding is never semantically required or byte-resolved; superseding binding is shaped but not resolved to a published release.
evidence_sha256 and action verification_policy_sha256 are signed declarations but no evidence root/catalog is accepted for actual-byte resolution.

No deterministic publisher observes current release/adoption/certificate/provider/policy/ontology state, derives exact transitions/actions, persists atomically, and returns a replay-safe publication receipt.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive fail-closed invalidation publisher and append-only ledger over the frozen v1 event, resolving exact live state and enforcing homogeneous targets, exact actions, rollback/supersession bytes, idempotency, and checkpoint continuity without modifying the frozen contract.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. They are disjoint from the held MF-P6-08.07 operational-invalidation unit, MF-P6-10.01-10.05 units, and preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty scope. Existing candidate paths are read-only; validation.py must not be mutated.
acceptance_criteria: Derive transitions only from observed current producer state and an exact accepted 08.07/10.05 authority decision; caller-supplied previous/new states cannot override observation.
Require every event to contain one homogeneous target kind and compatible scope class; mixed target kinds or unrelated scopes split into separately signed ordered events.
Map each reason to exactly—not at least—the allowed action multiset and exact transition coverage; extra, missing, duplicate, or inapplicable actions fail.
Resolve evidence, verification policy, superseding release, and rollback bindings to contained regular catalog bytes and current published identities before signing.
Bind stream, sequence, prior event ID/hash, causation, current journal checkpoint, and revocation head; publish by atomic append plus receipt-last checkpoint update.
Maintain an idempotency ledger where same-key/same-canonical-body returns the existing result and same-key/different-body fails without mutation.
Detect fork, deletion, insertion, reorder, replay across stream/epoch, stale head, concurrent append, and signer/trust/revocation drift while preserving immutable historical evidence.

Emit a strict self-hashed publication evidence record containing observed inputs, event payload/hash/signature, append position, previous/new checkpoint, verification results, and rollback/supersession resolution.

Tests cover all 27 reasons, mixed targets, exact actions, missing rollback, false supersession, evidence-byte drift, duplicate/collision idempotency, concurrent writers, fork/delete/reorder, stale trust/revocation, and crash at every append/checkpoint boundary.
Frozen v1 schemas/fixtures, held worker units, and dirty MF-P6-08.05 paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the frozen event/schema/validator and signed lifecycle-chain foundations only. It does not publish an invalidation, apply downstream actions, prove idempotent append/recovery, complete MF-P6-10.06, or claim production authority.
